column6: english translation ( Henry Wadsworth Longfellow (English, 1867)

column5:
Godi, Fiorenza, poi che se' sì grande
 che per mare e per terra batti l'ali,
 e per lo 'nferno tuo nome si spande!
column6:
Rejoice, O Florence, since thou art so great,
  That over sea and land thou beatest thy wings,
  And throughout Hell thy name is spread abroad!
column10: Modern city centroid.

column5:
Ma se presso al mattin del ver si sogna,
 tu sentirai, di qua da picciol tempo,
 di quel che Prato, non ch'altri, t'agogna.
column6:
But if when morn is near our dreams are true,
  Feel shalt thou in a little time from now
  What Prato, if none other, craves for thee.
column10: Modern city centroid.
Modern city centroid. Rome functions as imperial and providential geography.

column6:
They built their city over those dead bones,
  And, after her who first the place selected,
  Mantua named it, without other omen.
column10: Modern city centroid.

column5:
Suso in Italia bella giace un laco,
 a piè de l'Alpe che serra Lamagna
 sovra Tiralli, c'ha nome Benaco.
column6:
Above in beauteous Italy lies a lake
  At the Alp's foot that shuts in Germany
  Over Tyrol, and has the name Benaco.
column10: Representative point near Lake Garda center.

column5:
Tosto che l'acqua a correr mette co,
 non più Benaco, ma Mencio si chiama
 fino a Govèrnol, dove cade in Po.
column6:
Soon as the water doth begin to run,
  No more Benaco is it called, but Mincio,
  Far as Governo, where it falls in Po.
column10: Representative point at/near Mantua because Dante’s passage connects Mincio and Mantua.

column5:
“In mezzo mar siede un paese guasto,”
 diss' elli allora, “che s'appella Creta,
 sotto 'l cui rege fu già 'l mondo casto.
column6:
"In the mid-sea there sits a wasted land,"
  Said he thereafterward, "whose name is Crete,
  Under whose king the world of old was chaste.
column10: Island centroid. Coordinates are representative, not a boundary.
Coordinate is for Mount Ida/Psiloritis area on Crete

column5:
Ma quello ingrato popolo maligno
 che discese di Fiesole ab antico,
 e tiene ancor del monte e del macigno,
column6:
But that ungrateful and malignant people,
  Which of old time from Fesole descended,
  And smacks still of the mountain and the granite,
column10: Modern town centroid.
Classical Thebes in Boeotia.

column5:
mi diparti' da Circe, che sottrasse
 me più d'un anno là presso a Gaeta,
 prima che sì Enëa la nomasse,
column6:
From Circe had departed, who concealed me
  More than a year there near unto Gaeta,
  Or ever yet Aeneas named it so,
column10: Modern city centroid.

column5:
L'un lito e l'altro vidi infin la Spagna,
 fin nel Morrocco, e l'isola d'i Sardi,
 e l'altre che quel mare intorno bagna.
column6:
Both of the shores I saw as far as Spain,
  Far as Morocco, and the isle of Sardes,
  And the others which that sea bathes round about.
column10: Country centroid. Dante uses Spain as a broad western limit, not a specific point.

column5:
L'un lito e l'altro vidi infin la Spagna,
 fin nel Morrocco, e l'isola d'i Sardi,
 e l'altre che quel mare intorno bagna.
column6:
Both of the shores I saw as far as Spain,
  Far as Morocco, and the isle of Sardes,
  And the others which that sea bathes round about.
column10: Country centroid.

column5:
Piangendo mi sgridò: “Perché mi peste?
 se tu non vieni a crescer la vendetta
 di Montaperti, perché mi moleste?”
column6:
Weeping he growled: "Why dost thou trample me?
  Unless thou comest to increase the vengeance
  of Montaperti, why dost thou molest me?"
column10: Approximate battle-site area near Siena/Castelnuovo Berardenga.

column6:
That man no farther onward should adventure.
  On the right hand behind me left I Seville,
  And on the other already had left Ceuta.
column10: Modern city centroid.

column6:
That man no farther onward should adventure.
  On the right hand behind me left I Seville,
  And on the other already had left Ceuta.
column10: Setta is usually identified with Ceuta, opposite Seville in Ulysses’ navigation passage.

column6:
Already had the sun the horizon reached
  Whose circle of meridian covers o'er
  Jerusalem with its most lofty point,
column10: Modern city coordinate; in Dante, Jerusalem also anchors sacred/cosmic orientation.

column5:
e canterò di quel secondo regno
 dove l'umano spirito si purga
 e di salire al ciel diventa degno.
column6:
And of that second kingdom will I sing
  Wherein the human spirit doth purge itself,
  And to ascend to heaven becometh worthy.
column10: Very approximate representative point for the river. Dante uses Ganges as cosmographic/eastern reference

column5:
Ahi serva Italia, di dolore ostello,
 nave sanza nocchiere in gran tempesta,
 non donna di province, ma bordello!
column6:
Ah! servile Italy, grief's hostelry!
  A ship without a pilot in great tempest!
  No Lady thou of Provinces, but brothel!
column10: Approximate Italy centroid.

column5:
Atene e Lacedemona, che fenno
 l'antiche leggi e furon sì civili,
 fecero al viver bene un picciol cenno
column6:
Athens and Lacedaemon, they who made
  The ancient laws, and were so civilized,
  Made towards living well a little sign
column10: Ancient/modern Athens coordinate.

column5:
Atene e Lacedemona, che fenno
 l'antiche leggi e furon sì civili,

column6:
Athens and Lacedaemon, they who made
  The ancient laws, and were so civilized,
  Made towards living well a little sign
column10: Ancient/modern Sparta coordinate.

column5:
E io: “Per mezza Toscana si spazia
 un fiumicel che nasce in Falterona,
 e cento miglia di corso nol sazia.
column10: Approximate summit area.

column5:
“Se ben lo 'ntendimento tuo accarno
 con lo 'ntelletto,” allora mi rispuose
 quei che diceva pria, “tu parli d'Arno.”
column6:
"If well thy meaning I can penetrate
  With intellect of mine," then answered me
  He who first spake, "thou speakest of the Arno."
column10: Representative point at Florence on the Arno. Dante’s passage follows the river’s moral geography

column5:
“ricorditi di me, che son la Pia;
 Siena mi fé, disfecemi Maremma:
 salsi colui che 'nnanellata pria
disposando m'avea con la sua gemma.”
column10: Modern city centroid.

column5:
“ricorditi di me, che son la Pia;
 Siena mi fé, disfecemi Maremma:
 salsi colui che 'nnanellata pria
disposando m'avea con la sua gemma.”
column10: Approximate regional point.

column5:
Quel che fé poi ch'elli uscì di Ravenna
 e saltò Rubicon, fu di tal volo,
 che nol seguiteria lingua né penna.
column6:
What it achieved when it had left Ravenna,
  And leaped the Rubicon, was such a flight
  That neither tongue nor pen could follow it.
column10: Modern city centroid.

column5:
Inver' la Spagna rivolse lo stuolo,
 poi ver' Durazzo, e Farsalia percosse
 sì ch'al Nil caldo si sentì del duolo.
column6:
Round towards Spain it wheeled its legions; then
  Towards Durazzo, and Pharsalia smote
  That to the calid Nile was felt the pain.
column10: Representative point at Cairo on/near the Nile.

column5:

column6:

column5:
Intra Tupino e l'acqua che discende
 del colle eletto dal beato Ubaldo,
 fertile costa d'alto monte pende,
column6:
Between Tupino and the stream that falls
  Down from the hill elect of blessed Ubald,
  A fertile slope of lofty mountain hangs,

column5:
onde Perugia sente freddo e caldo
 da Porta Sole; e di rietro le piange
 per grave giogo Nocera con Gualdo.
column6:
From which Perugia feels the cold and heat
  Through Porta Sole, and behind it weep
  Gualdo and Nocera their grievous yoke.

column5:
Però chi d'esso loco fa parole,
 non dica Ascesi, ché direbbe corto,
 ma Orïente, se proprio dir vuole.
column6:
Therefore let him who speaketh of that place,
  Say not Ascesi, for he would say little,
  But Orient, if he properly would speak.

column5:
Di questa costa, là dov' ella frange
 più sua rattezza, nacque al mondo un sole,
 come fa questo talvolta di Gange.

column10: Approximate representative point for the Indus River region. Dante’s phrase is remote/world-scale, not a precise locality.

column5:
O beata Ungheria, se non si lascia
 più malmenare! e beata Navarra,
 se s'armasse del monte che la fascia!
column6:
O happy Hungary, if she let herself
  Be wronged no farther! and Navarre the happy,
  If with the hills that gird her she be armed!
column10: Country centroid. Broad political region.

column5:
O beata Ungheria, se non si lascia
 più malmenare! e beata Navarra,
 se s'armasse del monte che la fascia!
column6:
O happy Hungary, if she let herself
  Be wronged no farther! and Navarre the happy,
  If with the hills that gird her she be armed!
column10: Regional centroid for Navarre. Medieval/political boundaries differ from modern ones.

column5:
Sì come ad Arli, ove Rodano stagna,
 sì com' a Pola, presso del Carnaro
 ch'Italia chiude e suoi termini bagna,
column6:
Even as at Arles, where stagnant grows the Rhone,
  Even as at Pola near to the Quarnaro,
  That shuts in Italy and bathes its borders,
column10: Arles / Alyscamps necropolis used in Dante’s cemetery simile

column5:
Sì come ad Arli, ove Rodano stagna,
 sì com' a Pola, presso del Carnaro
 ch'Italia chiude e suoi termini bagna,
column6:
Even as at Arles, where stagnant grows the Rhone,
  Even as at Pola near to the Quarnaro,
  That shuts in Italy and bathes its borders,
column10: Pola = Pula, Istria, paired with Arles in the tombs simile

column5:
Qual è quella ruina che nel fianco
 di qua da Trento l'Adice percosse,
 o per tremoto o per sostegno manco,
column6:
Such as that ruin is which in the flank
  Smote, on this side of Trent, the Adige,
  Either by earthquake or by failing stay,
column10: Trent / Trento in the landslide simile above the violent river.

column5:
Qual è quella ruina che nel fianco
 di qua da Trento l'Adice percosse,
 o per tremoto o per sostegno manco,
column6:
Such as that ruin is which in the flank
  Smote, on this side of Trent, the Adige,
  Either by earthquake or by failing stay,
column10: Adige = Etsch; representative point near Trento for Dante’s simile.

column5:
Quale del Bulicame esce ruscello
 che parton poi tra lor le peccatrici,
 tal per la rena giù sen giva quello.
column6:
As from the Bulicame springs the brooklet,
  The sinful women later share among them,
  So downward through the sand it went its way.
column10: Thermal spring near Viterbo used to explain infernal hydrology; conservative modern spring location.

column5:
qui si nuota altrimenti che nel Serchio!
 Però, se tu non vuo' di nostri graffi,
 non far sopra la pegola soverchio.”
column10: Representative point near Lucca on the Serchio, named in the barrators episode.

column5:
così vid' ïo già temer li fanti
 ch'uscivan patteggiati di Caprona,
 veggendo sé tra nemici cotanti.
column6:
And thus beheld I once afraid the soldiers
  Who issued under safeguard from Caprona,
  Seeing themselves among so many foes.
column10: Fortified Caprona near Pisa, recalled from Dante’s own military experience.

column5:
Usa con esso donno Michel Zanche
 di Logodoro; e a dir di Sardigna
 le lingue lor non si sentono stanche.
column6:
Foregathers with him one Don Michael Zanche
  Of Logodoro; and of Sardinia
  To gossip never do their tongues feel tired.
column10: Island invoked through Michele Zanche and fraud in the fifth bolgia

column5:
Ahi Pisa, vituperio de le genti
 del bel paese là dove 'l sì suona,
 poi che i vicini a te punir son lenti,
column6:
Ah! Pisa, thou opprobrium of the people
  Of the fair land there where the 'Si' doth sound,
  Since slow to punish thee thy neighbours are,
column10: Ugolino’s denunciation of Pisa; distinct from Montaperti

column5:
"Oh!” rispuos' elli, “a piè del Casentino
 traversa un'acqua c'ha nome l'Archiano,
 che sovra l'Ermo nasce in Apennino.
column6:
"Oh," he replied, "at Casentino's foot
  A river crosses named Archiano, born
  Above the Hermitage in Apennine.
column10: Representative point for the Archiano near its confluence with the Arno in Bonconte’s narration.

column5:
"Oh!” rispuos' elli, “a piè del Casentino
 traversa un'acqua c'ha nome l'Archiano,
 che sovra l'Ermo nasce in Apennino.
column6:
"Oh," he replied, "at Casentino's foot
  A river crosses named Archiano, born
  Above the Hermitage in Apennine.
column10: Casentino valley named in Bonconte’s narrative; representative regional centroid.

column5:
E io a lui: “Qual forza o qual ventura
 ti travïò sì fuor di Campaldino,
 che non si seppe mai tua sepultura?”
column6:
And I to him: "What violence or what chance
  Led thee astray so far from Campaldino,
  That never has thy sepulture been known?"
column10: Battlefield of Campaldino, a major autobiographical site for Dante.
San Leo / Sanleo in Dante’s ascent simile.
Steep coastal Noli in the climbers’ simile.
Pietra di Bismantova; dramatic ascent comparator in Purgatorio IV.
Padua appears in Cunizza’s prophecy about the Veneto.

column6:
And thus thinks not the present multitude
  Shut in by Adige and Tagliamento,
  Nor yet for being scourged is penitent.
column10: Representative point in the middle Tagliamento basin; region border marker in Cunizza’s speech.

column5:
e dove Sile e Cagnan s'accompagna,
 tal signoreggia e va con la testa alta,
 che già per lui carpir si fa la ragna.
column6:
And where the Sile and Cagnano join
  One lordeth it, and goes with lofty head,
  For catching whom e'en now the net is making.
column10: Sile in the famous Treviso topographic marker.

column5:
e dove Sile e Cagnan s'accompagna,
 tal signoreggia e va con la testa alta,
 che già per lui carpir si fa la ragna.
column6:
And where the Sile and Cagnano join
  One lordeth it, and goes with lofty head,
  For catching whom e'en now the net is making.
column10: Cagnan / Botteniga-Cagnan system at Treviso; use confluence-area point with medium certainty.

column5:
Di quella valle fu' io litorano
 tra Ebro e Macra, che per cammin corto
 parte lo Genovese dal Toscano.
column6:
I was a dweller on that valley's shore
  'Twixt Ebro and Magra that with journey short
  Doth from the Tuscan part the Genoese.
column10: Magra / Macra river in Folquet’s western-Italian geography; representative river-mouth point.

column5:
e fanno un gibbo che si chiama Catria,
 di sotto al quale è consecrato un ermo,
 che suole esser disposto a sola latria.”
column6:
And form a ridge that Catria is called,
  'Neath which is consecrate a hermitage
  Wont to be dedicate to worship only."
column10: Mount Catria above Fonte Avellana; mountain anchor for Peter Damian’s description.
